disciplines.name: Quietus
disciplines.notes: Signature Discipline of the Assamite Warriors, focused on silent killing, blood manipulation, and precise, lethal strikes.

powers.name: Silence of Death
powers.system_text: Allows the Assamite to suppress sound in their immediate vicinity, making movement and combat unnaturally quiet.
powers.vbn_notes: Ideal for stealth scenes, rooftop hunts, and quiet confrontations where discovery would be disastrous.

powers.name: Scorpion's Touch
powers.system_text: Enables the vampire to turn their vitae into a paralytic or damaging toxin, delivered through blades or touch.
powers.vbn_notes: A narrative excuse for poisoned attacks and terrifying 'one-cut' executions.

disciplines.name: Assamite Sorcery
disciplines.notes: The blood magic tradition of the Assamite Sorcerers, built on complex hierarchical paths and rituals distinct from Tremere thaumaturgy.

disciplines.name: Celerity
disciplines.notes: Supernatural speed and reaction; in Assamites, it often appears as blindingly fast, precise strikes and the ability to be in the right place at the right time.

disciplines.name: Obfuscate
disciplines.notes: The ability to vanish from notice or blend into surroundings, used heavily by Warriors and infiltrator Viziers.

disciplines.name: Auspex
disciplines.notes: Heightened perception, aura reading, and supernatural insight; often emphasized by Viziers and Sorcerers.

rituals.name: Blood Oath of the True Name
rituals.system_text: A ritual in which the Sorcerer binds a target's true name to an oath, allowing mystical repercussions if the oath is broken.
rituals.vbn_notes: Can be used in VbN as a justification for narrative consequences and hooks when PCs or NPCs betray accords.

rituals.name: Veil of Haqim
rituals.system_text: Conceals an Assamite or a small group from mystical and mundane detection for a time, at the cost of rare components and careful preparation.
rituals.vbn_notes: Explains why some operations are 'untraceable' except through story investigation, not raw Disciplines.

backgrounds.name: Clan Mentor
backgrounds.system_text: Represents the guidance and protection of a more experienced member of the Warrior, Sorcerer, or Vizier castes.
backgrounds.vbn_notes: In VbN, this Background can serve as a pipeline for missions, warnings, and internal clan complications.

backgrounds.name: Safe Houses and Strongholds
backgrounds.system_text: A network of secure locations maintained by the clan for shelter, training, and clandestine meetings.
backgrounds.vbn_notes: Can be mapped to Haven-related merits or narrative access to fortified locations in the Valley by Night setting.

merits_flaws.name: Code of Haqim
merits_flaws.system_text: The character strongly adheres to a codified set of rules regarding who may be killed and under what circumstances, granting mental stability but limiting opportunistic violence.
merits_flaws.vbn_notes: Justifies disciplined behavior and gives STs leverage to penalize or reward adherence in narrative terms.

merits_flaws.name: Blood Addiction (Kindred Vitae)
merits_flaws.system_text: The Assamite struggles with a strong craving for other vampires' blood, making self-control difficult when exposed to opportunities for diablerie or excessive feeding.
merits_flaws.vbn_notes: A key flaw to highlight the clan’s historical reputation and internal tension over diablerie.

other_systems.name: Clan Weakness – Blood Hunger for the Damned
other_systems.system_text: Historically, Assamites develop an unhealthy attraction to the vitae of other Kindred, and their curses or rituals often interact with this hunger.
other_systems.vbn_notes: In VbN, this should surface as story pressure rather than a purely mechanical penalty, tempting Assamites in critical scenes.

other_systems.name: Caste Distinctions
other_systems.system_text: Warriors, Sorcerers, and Viziers each have different expected roles, favored Disciplines, and internal obligations within the clan.
other_systems.vbn_notes: Useful as tags for NPC generation and as flags for how other Assamites and outside factions respond to the character.
